text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-off rotation, defensives bid, AI complex hit. Profit-taking in technology and AI infrastructure following recent record highs met a hawkish macro backdrop with CPI YoY at 3.95% and PCE YoY at 3.50%, pushing investors into energy, staples, and financials. Higher-for-longer expectations with Fed funds at 3.63% and MOVE at 86.1 kept pressure on long duration and high-multiple names like Nvidia, Intel, AMD, and Micron while energy leadership rode USO’s +0.72% and XLE’s +1.92% gains.

INDICES — VIX closed at 17.8, down vs the 20-day MA of 18.0. SPY -0.07%, QQQ -0.43%, IWM -0.59%, DIA +0.33% ranked DIA > IJR > SPY > QQQ > IWM on the broader list, with STYLE showing SMALL > LARGE by 0.42pp. SPY remains above its 200-day MA near 6,950, while QQQ also sits above its 200-day MA near 24,000, keeping CTAs engaged on the long side. The most notable cross-asset move was USO +0.72% alongside TLT -0.12%, reinforcing higher-real-rate pressure.

VOLUME — RSP volume at 1.6x and DIA at 1.3x with both up (RSP +0.62%, DIA +0.33%) signaled accumulation in equal weight and industrial-heavy large caps. QQQ volume at 1.3x with price -0.43% flagged distribution in growth/tech, while SPY at 0.8x with price -0.07% showed a low-conviction index pullback. XLK at 1.7x with price -1.08% underscored targeted tech selling, while XLU at 1.5x with price +0.16% showed defensive accumulation.

SECTORS — XLE Energy +1.92%, XLP Staples +1.49%, XLF Financials +1.25%, XLRE Real Estate +1.20%, XLC Comms +0.78%, XLV Healthcare +0.43%, XLU Utilities +0.16%, XLB Materials -0.16%, XLY Discretionary -0.18%, XLI Industrials -0.38%, XLK Technology -1.08%. Top industries were MSOS +4.54% and XES +3.06%, while bottom industries were IBIT -2.88% and TAN -3.62%.

THE 7s — Software +3.42% (strongest NOW), Medtech +2.91% (strongest DXCM), Insurance +2.27% (strongest TRV), Toll Road +2.16% (strongest FER), Defense +1.86% (strongest LMT). Buildout -4.17% (weakest VRT), Fab -3.61% (weakest AMAT), New Metals -3.30% (weakest MP), Semi -1.11% (weakest NVDA), Mag -0.69% (weakest TSLA). Software showed internal dispersion with NOW +8–10% while laggards in legacy names sat near flat.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC is June 17 with CME probabilities hold=100%, cut=0%, hike=0%, and stance tally Hawk=6, Neutral=5, Dove=4 confirming a hawkish-leaning but data-dependent committee. USD liquidity stress at 39/100 labeled Normal, with Reserve Scarcity at the 79th percentile the most stressed component. TED spread at 0.09% and ON-RRP at $7.2B kept money-market conditions benign despite a TGA level of $838,584M.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR at 0.71 with ATM IV at 16.3% and skew +9.9% indicated mild complacency in broad equity with some put demand. QQQ PCR at 1.53 and IWM PCR at 1.63 with positive skew (QQQ +8.2%, IWM +6.4%) reflected elevated protection demand in growth and small caps. VIX at 17.8 just below its 20-day MA of 18.0 and MOVE at 86.1 versus 72.1 highlighted bond vol stress outpacing equity vol.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil via USO +0.72% and XLE +1.92% rallied while XLI -0.38% and cyclical industrials like Caterpillar lagged, showing higher energy costs pressuring traditional cyclicals. UUP -0.25% fell while GLD +0.27% and SLV +1.30% bounced, suggesting some inflation-hedge demand despite the hawkish macro tone.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed at 56.7/100 sat Neutral. Biggest movers included NOW in Software +8–10%, NEE in Power -4.63%, and VRT in Buildout roughly flat to slightly negative despite earlier strength around +11.3% off lows. Best 20d momentum sector was XLK +12.81% and worst was XLU -3.96%, while best basket was Semi +32.01% and worst was Housing -11.83%. NEE with 4.4x volume and price -4.63% showed clear distribution in Power, while EWZ with 2.4x volume and -3.82% flagged EM de-risking, both tied to rate and commodity volatility.

WATCH — NEE -4.63% deserves attention given 4.4x volume, sector underperformance in Power -0.58%, and potential follow-through if rates or regulatory headlines worsen. VRT around -1 to -2% on the day despite intraday strength to $368.01 and inclusion in Buildout -4.17% merits focus as a sentiment tell for AI infrastructure. NOW +8–10% on Software +3.42% with likely earnings or AI workflow catalysts and strong volume could become a new software leadership marker or a fade candidate if macro headwinds resume.
